title: astragalus protractus
intro: Astragalus protractus, also known as milk vetch or astragalus, is a type of perennial legume native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of the Himalayas and Tibet. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of astragalus protractus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and fatigue, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. Astragalus protractus is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
